title: PBMC Isolation from Apheresis Collars
author: Girija Goyal
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.56bg9an

steps.title: Acquire collar and make an incision...
steps.instruction: Acquire collar and make an incision to drain blood into 50mL conical tube.

steps.title: Dilute blood product with 2X volume RPMI or PBS
steps.instruction: Dilute blood product with 2X volume RPMI or PBS. Mix well.

steps.title: Slowly layer solution on top of...
steps.instruction: Slowly layer solution on top of 10 mL density gradient solution.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300 g for 25 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300 g for 25 minutes at room temperature. Set acceleration and deceleration levels to minimal. Undetermined

steps.title: Remove white layer of PBMCs using...
steps.instruction: Remove white layer of PBMCs using a 5 mL pipette tip.

steps.title: Add these cells to 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Add these cells to 10 mL warm media in a 50 mL tube.

steps.title: If using 5 ml or more...
steps.instruction: If using 5 ml or more of the Leukopak, you may have a very high number of cells. To effectively wash them, fill tube to 50 mL.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 120 g for 10...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 120 g for 10 minutes to remove platelets and get an accurate count. Return acceleration / deceleration levels to high or 9.

steps.title: Aspirate media and resuspend cells in...
steps.instruction: Aspirate media and resuspend cells in 20 mL warm media per 10 ml of starting blood product. Steps 10-12 can be optimized depending on your yield.

steps.title: Dilute 100x by adding 10ul cell...
steps.instruction: Dilute 100x by adding 10ul cell solutions to 890 ul media in a 1ml eppendorf tube and 100ul Trypan blue

steps.title: Count cells using a hemocytometer
steps.instruction: Count cells using a hemocytometer. Count the number of cells in each of the four quadrants. Use the following formula to find the total number of cells.total # of cells = (cells counted /4)X dilution factorX 104 cells/ml

steps.title: Cells can be kept in solution...
steps.instruction: Cells can be kept in solution in the refrigerator for up to two hours.
